description: OutSplice is a package that compares alternative splicing events between tumor and normal samples. This package is specifically designed for analyzing the gene and junction information from RNA sequencing data provided by the user, or from the TCGA. This package generates a matrix of splicing outliers, which are junctions that are either significantly over or under-expressed compared to a control sample. OutSplice further designates observed outliers as skipping, insertion, or deletion events. Overall, OutSplice is novel in that it determines differential splicing burdens between tumors and normal samples and characterizes the nature of splicing outliers.
author: Joseph Bendik;UCSD
plan:
## OutSplice GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
OutSplice is a GenePattern module that OutSplice is a package that compares alternative splicing events between tumor and normal samples. This package is specifically designed for analyzing the gene and junction information from RNA sequencing data provided by the user, or from the TCGA. This package generates a matrix of splicing outliers, which are junctions that are either significantly over or under-expressed compared to a control sample. OutSplice further designates observed outliers as skipping, insertion, or deletion events. Overall, OutSplice is novel in that it determines differential splicing burdens between tumors and normal samples and characterizes the nature of splicing outliers.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 14 parameters:
- Required: junction.file, gene.expression.file, rawcounts.file, out.file.prefix, filter.sex, offsets.value, correction.setting, p.value, genome.annotations, use_junc_col, use_gene_col, use_rc_col, use_labels_col
- Optional: sample.labels.file

### 3. Command Line
```
Rscript /build/source/outsplice_wrapper.R <junction.file> <gene.expression.file> <rawcounts.file>     <out.file.prefix> <filter.sex>    <offsets.value> <correction.setting> <p.value>   <sample.labels.file>   <genome.annotations> <use_junc_col> <use_gene_col> <use_rc_col> <use_labels_col>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/outsplice\:0.4

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: junction.file
parameters.description: Matrix of junction raw read counts

parameters.name: gene.expression.file
parameters.description: Matrix of normalized gene expression data. RSEM quartile normalized data is recommended.

parameters.name: rawcounts.file
parameters.description: Matrix of raw read counts for each gene. Can either be per gene, or a summed total for each sample.

parameters.name: sample.labels.file
parameters.description: Matrix of phenotypes, 2 columns with sample name in column 1 and either T or N in column two (for tumor or normal).

parameters.name: out.file.prefix
parameters.description: Prefix that will be used for all output files.

parameters.name: filter.sex
parameters.description: Ignores sex chromosomes when generating results. True by default.

parameters.name: offsets.value
parameters.description: The normalized junction expression threshold. Uses 0.00001 by default.

parameters.name: correction.setting
parameters.description: The correction value to be used for p-value adjustment during Fisher analyses. Uses fdr by default. Allowed values are fdr, holm, hochberg, hommel, bonferonni, BY.

parameters.name: p.value
parameters.description: The final corrected significance threshold to use during Fisher analyses. Uses 0.05 by default.

parameters.name: genome.annotations
parameters.description: Genome and transcript annotations to use.

parameters.name: use_junc_col
parameters.description: An integer indicating which column in the junction matrix contains the junction regions in your matrices. Uses 1 by default.

parameters.name: use_gene_col
parameters.description: .An integer indicating which column in the gene_expr matrix contains the entrez ids of your genes Uses 1 by default.

parameters.name: use_rc_col
parameters.description: An integer indicating which column in the rawcounts matrix contains the row names. Uses 1 by default.

parameters.name: use_labels_col
parameters.description: An integer indicating which column in the sample_labels matrix contains the sample names. Uses 1 by default.
